question.text: High Frequency Trading (HFT) in commodity markets is characterized by:

answer.text: Latency-sensitive trading strategies that are part of algorithmic trading with order frequency above 10 OPS
Trading once per week based on fundamental analysis
Manual order placement by experienced traders
Only hedging strategies, not speculative trading

explanation.text: **The Logic:** HFT is part of algorithmic trading comprising latency-sensitive strategies. HFT algos have > 10 OPS (Orders Per Second). They exploit tiny price discrepancies at high speed. **The Trap:** HFT is high-speed, not weekly. It's algorithmic, not manual. It includes speculative strategies. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers HFT regulations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Exchanges impose penalty provisions at different slabs of Order-to-Trade ratio to discourage excessive order submissions that can destabilize the market.
